examples: Fail—"a noun or interjection used when something is egregiously unsuccessful"—was 2009's the most useful word.
Reform has been vigorously opposed, perhaps most egregiously in education, where charter schools, academies and merit pay all faced drawn-out battles.
Judicial figures have meddled egregiously in economic affairs before.
Most egregiously, Mr. Osborne last month announced a program to subsidize the riskiest mortgages.
There were curried and primavera versions, and an egregiously un-kosher one fried in bacon fat.
